steps.title: Split the absolute value equation
steps.content:
The equation $|x - 6| = 13$ means the distance from $x$ to 6 is 13. This gives two cases:
$x - 6 = 13$ or $x - 6 = -13$

Solving each:
  • From the first: $x = 19$
  • From the second: $x = -7$

steps.title: Use symmetry to find the sum
steps.content:
The two solutions are symmetric around the center value 6. Notice that $19 = 6 + 13$ and $-7 = 6 - 13$. Therefore, their sum is:
$$19 + (-7) = 12$$

This can also be seen directly: for $|x - a| = b$, the solutions always sum to $2a$. In our problem with $a = 6$, we get $2(6) = 12$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
